title: Case Studies | Psychology Direct Expert Witness & Education Services
description: Real examples of how expert witness assessments and educational psychology changed case outcomes. Family law, criminal, education, and more.

blocks.headline: Proven Results Across Legal and Education
blocks.subtitle: Real examples across family law, criminal law, civil claims, and education. See the impact of expert evidence and how we managed complexity.

tabs.label: Legal Cases
tabs.content:
**Complex Logistics in a Family Law Case**

A specialist family law firm needed assessments of a large family across multiple locations, involving domestic violence and tight court timelines. We matched two psychologists, coordinated all scheduling, and delivered reports on time. Children returned home on a supervision order with clear support plans.

**Fitness to Plead Assessment — Urgent Deadline**

A criminal defence solicitor had 14 days for a fitness to plead assessment. Within 4 hours, we matched a psychiatrist with expertise in fitness to plead and intellectual disability. The assessment was completed within 5 days. Trial proceeded successfully.

**Care Proceedings — Psychiatric Assessment**

A Local Authority legal team needed a psychiatric assessment of a mother's parenting capacity and treatment response. We matched a GMC-registered psychiatrist with Section 12 approval. The report provided clear evidence for the court's decision.

**Criminal Defence — Dangerousness and Risk Assessment**

A defence solicitor needed a psychological risk assessment for sentencing. We matched a forensic psychologist who assessed trauma history, risk factors, and rehabilitation potential. The judge imposed a community order instead of custody.

**Personal Injury — Psychological Injury Assessment**

A PI solicitor needed expert evidence for a psychological injury case. We matched a clinical psychologist who assessed PTSD, quantified functional impairment, and provided long-term support prognosis. The expert report supported a damages settlement reflecting true long-term care costs.

**Immigration — Asylum Claim with Torture Survivor**

An immigration solicitor represented a torture survivor. We matched a psychiatrist and a psychologist who together assessed medical and psychological sequelae of torture. The tribunal recognised credibility and granted asylum protection.

**Employment Tribunal — Mental Health Discrimination**

An employment solicitor handled a discrimination claim. We matched a clinical psychologist with occupational health expertise who assessed mental health, functional impact, and employer accommodation duty. The tribunal found in favour of the claimant.

tabs.label: Education Cases
tabs.content:
**County Council Faces Major EHCP Assessment Backlog**

A large Local Authority was facing a backlog of 40+ EHCP assessments with statutory deadlines approaching. We provided 4 additional EPs on temporary contract. The LA cleared the backlog within 6 weeks, met all statutory timelines, and avoided legal challenge.

**Primary School Needed EP Report Before End of Term**

A primary school needed an EP assessment for a child with suspected dyslexia. We matched an EP with dyslexia specialism, completed the assessment within 10 days and the report within 2 weeks. The child received immediate support and clear transition planning.

**Multi-Academy Trust — EP Service for 5 Schools**

A MAT with 5 schools needed ongoing EP support but couldn't afford full-time staff. We provided a shared EP working 0.5 FTE across the MAT, coordinated by our Client Manager. All 5 schools received consistent EP support. The MAT renewed year-on-year.

**Full EP Service for Large Local Authority**

A Local Authority lacked sufficient EP capacity. We provided comprehensive EP service: dedicated EPs, surge capacity, and specialist EPs for complex assessments. The LA met all statutory timelines and established a 3-year contract.

**Exam Access Arrangements — Psychometric Assessment for GCSE**

A secondary school had a student with suspected specific learning difficulties needing exam access arrangements. We matched an EP with psychometric expertise who provided a JCQ-compliant report. The student received exam access arrangements and achieved predicted grades.

blocks.heading: See Your Case Here?
blocks.bodyText: Our case studies show real outcomes across legal and education sectors. If you have a similar case, our Client Managers can discuss how we've helped.

faqs.question: Can you share names and identifying details?
faqs.answer: We protect client confidentiality. Case studies are anonymised and focus on challenge, approach, and outcomes. Details are changed to protect privacy.

faqs.question: Are these case studies representative of typical cases?
faqs.answer: Yes and no. We showcase diverse case types to show breadth of experience. Some are more complex than typical, others more routine. Use them to see what's possible.

faqs.question: Can I speak to a past client?
faqs.answer: We can provide client references for specific case types. Contact our team at /contact/ or call 01306 879 975 to request a reference.

faqs.question: Do you have case studies in my specific area of law or education?
faqs.answer: We work across family law, criminal law, civil law, employment law, immigration law, and education. If you don't see your case type, call us. We likely have relevant experience.

faqs.question: What was the outcome in a specific case study?
faqs.answer: Each case study includes the outcome achieved. For more detail, contact us and we can discuss that specific case type.

blocks.heading: Ready to Instruct an Expert?
blocks.bodyText: Our track record shows we deliver results. Your Client Manager will discuss your situation and match you with proven expertise.
